<div id="content-area"
class="relative grow box-border flex-col w-full mx-auto px-1 lg:pl-[23.7rem] lg:-ml-12 xl:w-[calc(100%-28rem)]">

<div id="header" class="relative leading-none">

<div class="mt-0.5 space-y-2.5">

<div class="flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-start sm:items-center relative gap-2 min-w-0">

# The MCP Registry

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] justify-end ml-auto sm:flex hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="flex items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] mt-3 sm:hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

</div>

<div id="content"
class="mdx-content @container/columns-container relative mt-8 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [contain:inline-size] isolate empty:hidden mb-14"
page-title="The MCP Registry" page-href="/registry/about">

<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20"
callout-type="note">

<div class="mt-0.5 w-4" component-part="callout-icon">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" />

</div>

<div class="text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&_code]:!text-current [&_kbd]:!text-current [&_a]:!text-current [&_a]:border-current [&_strong]:!text-current text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300"
component-part="callout-content">

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry is currently in preview. Breaking
changes or data resets may occur before general availability. If you
encounter any issues, please report them on
<a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/issues"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">GitHub</a>.</span>

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry is the official centralized metadata
repository for publicly accessible MCP servers, backed by major trusted
contributors to the MCP ecosystem such as Anthropic, GitHub, PulseMCP,
and Microsoft.</span> <span data-as="p">The MCP Registry
provides:</span>

- A single place for server creators to publish metadata about their
  servers
- Namespace management through DNS verification
- A REST API for MCP clients and aggregators to discover available
  servers
- Standardized installation and configuration information

<span data-as="p">Server metadata is stored in a standardized <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/blob/main/docs/reference/server-json/server.schema.json"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer"><code>server.json</code>
format</a>, which contains:</span>

- The server’s unique name (e.g., `io.github.user/server-name`)
- Where to locate the server (e.g., npm package name, remote server URL)
- Execution instructions (e.g., command-line args, env vars)
- Other discovery data (e.g., description, server capabilities)

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#the-mcp-registry-ecosystem"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">The MCP Registry Ecosystem</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry is part of an ecosystem that looks
something like:</span> <span aria-owns="rmiz-modal-"
rmiz=""><span rmiz-content="not-found" style="visibility:visible"><img
src="https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=06fcebbf5a33d3ef1fed72279f01f321"
class="object-contain" style="aspect-ratio:2787 / 1811"
data-path="registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg" decoding="async"
sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px"
srcset="https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?w=280&amp;fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=32fbfc2a788116d4ddec8f3528bc08f5 280w, https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?w=560&amp;fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=e827d2dcd6bb208f36ae321c8ec093ae 560w, https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?w=840&amp;fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=d52fed86aaeb84876c12604436f6ccf2 840w, https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?w=1100&amp;fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=94c0d91fe85c6ec34df3308ded052653 1100w, https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?w=1650&amp;fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=e684efea275a1084d76ca1b8b790334d 1650w, https://mintcdn.com/mcp/jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo/registry/ecosystem-diagram.excalidraw.svg?w=2500&amp;fit=max&amp;auto=format&amp;n=jWyXLg8iLYJ-g6Lo&amp;q=85&amp;s=e094586b018e8a008b08a4d804fd7d19 2500w"
data-optimize="true" width="2787" height="1811"
alt="The MCP Registry ecosystem" /></span></span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#relationship-with-package-registries"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Relationship with Package Registries</span>

<span data-as="p">Package registries — such as npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub
— host packages with code and binaries.</span> <span data-as="p">The MCP
Registry hosts metadata that points to those packages.</span>
<span data-as="p">For example, a `weather-mcp` package could be hosted
on npm, and metadata in the MCP Registry could map the “weather v1.2.0”
server to `npm:weather-mcp`.</span> <span data-as="p">The
<a href="package-types.html" class="link">Package Types guide</a> lists
the supported package types and registries. More package registries may
be supported in the future based on community demand. If you are
interested in building support for a package registry, please
<a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">open an issue</a>.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#relationship-with-server-developers"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Relationship with Server Developers</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry supports both open-source and
closed-source servers. Server developers can publish their server’s
metadata to the registry as long as the server’s installation method is
publicly available (e.g., an npm package or a Docker image on a public
registry) *or* the server itself is publicly accessible (e.g., a remote
server that is not restricted to private networks).</span>
<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry **does not** support private servers.
Private servers are those that are only accessible to a narrow set of
users. For example, servers published on a private network (like
`mcp.acme-corp.internal`) or on private package registries (e.g.
`npx -y @acme/mcp --registry https://artifactory.acme-corp.internal/npm`).
If you want to publish private servers, we recommend that you host your
own private MCP registry and add them there.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#relationship-with-downstream-aggregators"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Relationship with Downstream
Aggregators</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry is intended to be consumed primarily
by downstream aggregators, such as MCP server marketplaces.</span>
<span data-as="p">The metadata hosted by the MCP Registry is
deliberately unopinionated. Downstream aggregators can provide curation
or additional metadata such as community ratings.</span>
<span data-as="p">We expect that downstream aggregators will use the MCP
Registry API to pull new metadata on a regular but infrequent basis (for
example, once per hour). See the
<a href="registry-aggregators.html" class="link">MCP Registry
Aggregators guide</a> for more information.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#relationship-with-other-mcp-registries"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Relationship with Other MCP
Registries</span>

<span data-as="p">In addition to a public REST API, the MCP Registry
defines an <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/blob/main/docs/reference/api/openapi.yaml"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OpenAPI spec</a> that
other MCP registries can implement in order to provide a standardized
interface for MCP host applications.</span> <span data-as="p">We expect
that many downstream aggregators will implement this interface. Private
MCP registries can implement it as well to benefit from existing host
application support.</span> <span data-as="p">Note that the official MCP
Registry codebase is **not** designed for self-hosting, and the registry
maintainers cannot provide support for this use case. If you choose to
fork it, you would need to maintain and operate it independently.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#relationship-with-mcp-host-applications"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Relationship with MCP Host
Applications</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry is not intended to be directly
consumed by host applications. Instead, host applications should consume
other MCP registries, such as downstream marketplaces, via a REST API
conforming to the official MCP Registry’s OpenAPI spec.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#trust-and-security"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Trust and Security</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#verifying-server-authenticity"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Verifying Server Authenticity</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry uses namespace authentication to
ensure that servers come from their claimed sources. Server names follow
a reverse DNS format (like `io.github.username/server` or
`com.example/server`) that ties them to verified GitHub accounts or
domains.</span> <span data-as="p">This namespace system ensures that
only the legitimate owner of a GitHub account or domain can publish
servers under that namespace, providing trust and accountability in the
ecosystem. For details on authentication methods, see the
<a href="authentication.html" class="link">Authentication guide</a>.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#security-scanning"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Security Scanning</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry delegates security scanning
to:</span>

- **Underlying package registries** — npm, PyPI, Docker Hub, and other
  package registries perform their own security scanning and
  vulnerability detection.
- **Downstream aggregators** — MCP Registry aggregators and marketplaces
  can implement additional security checks, ratings, or curation.

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry focuses on namespace authentication
and metadata hosting, while relying on the broader ecosystem for
security scanning of actual server code.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="about.html#spam-prevention"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Spam Prevention</span>

<span data-as="p">The MCP Registry uses multiple mechanisms to prevent
spam:</span>

- **Namespace authentication requirements** — Publishers must verify
  ownership of their namespace through GitHub, DNS, or HTTP challenges,
  preventing arbitrary spam submissions.
- **Character limits and validation** — Free-form fields have strict
  character limits and regex validation to prevent abuse.
- **Manual takedown** — The registry maintainers can manually remove
  spam or malicious servers. See the
  <a href="moderation-policy.html" class="link">Moderation Policy</a>
  for details on what content is removed.

<span data-as="p">Future spam prevention measures under consideration
include stricter rate limiting, AI-based spam detection, and community
reporting capabilities.</span>

</div>

<div class="feedback-toolbar pb-16 w-full flex flex-col gap-y-8">
